name: MATHEMATICS
question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/5/3 (set-3)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 7 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. This question paper contains three Sections - A, B and C.
  2. Each section is compulsory.
  3. Section-A has 6 short-answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section-B has 4 short-answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section-C has 4 long-answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question 14 is a case study based question with two subparts of 2 marks each.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

The Cartesian equation of a line AB is: {tex}\frac{2 x-1}{12}=\frac{y+2}{2}=\frac{z-3}{3}{/tex} Find the direction cosines of a line parallel to line AB.

 2
2.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{4 x-x^2}}{/tex}

 2
3.(i)

Events A and B are such that P(A) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}, P(B) = {tex}\frac{7}{12}{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{P}(\bar{\mathrm{A}} \cup \bar{\mathrm{B}})=\frac{1}{4}{/tex} Find whether the events A and B are independent or not.

 2

OR
(ii)

A box B1 contains 1 white ball and 3 red balls. Another box B2 contains 2 white balls and 3 red balls. If one ball is drawn at random from each of the boxes B1 and B2, then find the probability that the two balls drawn are of the same colour.

 2
4.

Find the general solution for differential equation:  {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex}

 2
5.

If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 1 and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}, then find {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex}

 2
6.

Let X be a random variable which assumes values x1, x2, x3, x4 such that 2P (X = x1) = 3P (X = x2) = P (X = x3) = 5P (X = x4). Find the probability distribution of X.

 2

SECTION B

Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.(i)

If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are two vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=|\vec{b}|{/tex}, then prove that {tex}(\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec {a}{/tex}.

 3

OR
(ii)

If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} are unit vectors and {tex}\theta{/tex} is the angle between them, then prove that {tex}\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|{/tex}.

 3
8.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex} 

 3
9.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_1^3 \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt{4-x}} d x{/tex}

 3
10.

Find the distance of the point (2, 3, 4) measured along the line {tex}\frac{x-4}{3}=\frac{y+5}{6}=\frac{z+1}{2}{/tex} from the plane 3x + 2y + 2z + 5 = 0.

 3

SECTION C

Question numbers 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.

Find the area bounded by the curves y = |x - 1| and y = 1, using integration.

 4
12.(i)

Solve the following differential equation: (y-sin2x)dx + tan x dy = 0.

 4

OR
(ii)

Find the general solution of the differential equation: (x3 + y3)dy = x2ydx

 4
13.

There are two boxes, namely box-I and box-II. Box-I contains 3 red and 6 black balls. Box-II contains 5 red and 5 black balls. One of the two boxes, is selected at random and a ball is drawn at random. The ball drawn is found to be red. Find the probability that this red ball comes out from box-II.

 4

Case Study Based Question
14.

Two motorcycles A and B are running at the speed more than the allowed speed on the roads represented by the lines {tex}\vec{r}=\lambda(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=(3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}){/tex} {tex}+\mu(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} respectively.

Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

Find the shortest distance between the given lines.

 2
(ii)

Find the point at which the motorcycles may collide.

 2

SECTION A Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each. 1. The Cartesian equation of a line AB is: {tex}\frac{2 x-1}{12}=\frac{y+2}{2}=\frac{z-3}{3}{/tex} Find the direction cosines of a line parallel to line AB. 2 2. Find: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{4 x-x^2}}{/tex} 2 3. (i) Events A and B are such that P(A) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} , P(B) = {tex}\frac{7}{12}{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{P}(\bar{\mathrm{A}} \cup \bar{\mathrm{B}})=\frac{1}{4}{/tex} Find whether the events A and B are independent or not. 2 OR (ii) A box B 1 contains 1 white ball and 3 red balls. Another box B 2 contains 2 white balls and 3 red balls. If one ball is drawn at random from each of the boxes B 1 and B 2 , then find the probability that the two balls drawn are of the same colour. 2 4. Find the general solution for differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex} 2 5. If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 1 and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} , then find {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex} 2 6. Let X be a random variable which assumes values x 1 , x 2 , x 3 , x 4 such that 2P (X = x 1 ) = 3P (X = x 2 ) = P (X = x 3 ) = 5P (X = x 4 ). Find the probability distribution of X. 2 SECTION B Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each. 7. (i) If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are two vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=|\vec{b}|{/tex} , then prove that {tex}(\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} . 3 OR (ii) If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} are unit vectors and {tex}\theta{/tex} is the angle between them, then prove that {tex}\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|{/tex} . 3 8. (i) Find: {tex}\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex} 3 OR (ii) Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex} 3 9. Find the distance of the point (2, 3, 4) measured along the line {tex}\frac{x-4}{3}=\frac{y+5}{6}=\frac{z+1}{2}{/tex} from the plane 3x + 2y + 2z + 5 = 0. 3 SECTION C Question numbers 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each. 11. Find the area bounded by the curves y = |x - 1| and y = 1, using integration. 4 12. (i) Solve the following differential equation: (y-sin 2 x)dx + tan x dy = 0. 4 OR (ii) Find the general solution of the differential equation: (x 3 + y 3 )dy = x 2 ydx 4 13. There are two boxes, namely box-I and box-II. Box-I contains 3 red and 6 black balls. Box-II contains 5 red and 5 black balls. One of the two boxes, is selected at random and a ball is drawn at random. The ball drawn is found to be red. Find the probability that this red ball comes out from box-II. 4 Case Study Based Question 14. Two motorcycles A and B are running at the speed more than the allowed speed on the roads represented by the lines {tex}\vec{r}=\lambda(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=(3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}){/tex} {tex}+\mu(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} respectively. Based on the above information, answer the following questions: (i) Find the shortest distance between the given lines. 2 (ii) Find the point at which the motorcycles may collide. 2
answer_key_html:
Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/5/3 (set-3)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 7 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. This question paper contains three Sections - A, B and C.
  2. Each section is compulsory.
  3. Section-A has 6 short-answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section-B has 4 short-answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section-C has 4 long-answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question 14 is a case study based question with two subparts of 2 marks each.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

The Cartesian equation of a line AB is: {tex}\frac{2 x-1}{12}=\frac{y+2}{2}=\frac{z-3}{3}{/tex} Find the direction cosines of a line parallel to line AB.

Solution:

We have, {tex}\frac{2 x-1}{12}=\frac{y+2}{2}=\frac{z-3}{3}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x-{\frac12}}{\frac{12}2}=\frac{y+2}2=\frac{z-3}3{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x-{\frac12}}6=\frac{y+2}2=\frac{z-3}3{/tex}
The direction ratios of the line parallel to AB are proportional to {tex}\to{/tex} 6, 2, 3
The direction cosines of the line parallel to AB are proportional to {tex}\to{/tex} {tex}\frac6{\sqrt{36+4+9}}=\frac2{\sqrt{36+4+9}}=\frac3{\sqrt{36+4+9}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac67=\frac27=\frac37{/tex}

 2
2.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{4 x-x^2}}{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\int\frac1{\sqrt{4x-x^2}}dx=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x\right)}}dx{/tex}
{tex}=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x+4-4\right)}}dx=\int\frac1{-\left(x-2\right)^2+4}dx{/tex}
{tex}=\int\frac1{\left(2\right)^2-\left(x-2\right)^2}dx{/tex}
Put x - 2 = t, dx = dt
{tex}=\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{dx}{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac xa\right)+C{/tex}
So, {tex}\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac t2\right)+C{/tex}
Putting value of t
{tex}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{x-2}2\right)+C{/tex}

 2
3.(i)

Events A and B are such that P(A) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}, P(B) = {tex}\frac{7}{12}{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{P}(\bar{\mathrm{A}} \cup \bar{\mathrm{B}})=\frac{1}{4}{/tex} Find whether the events A and B are independent or not.

Solution:

P(A) = {tex}\frac12{/tex}, P(B) = {tex}\frac7{12}{/tex}, {tex}P\left(A'\cup B'\right)=\frac14{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}P\left(A'\cup B'\right)=P\left(A\cap B\right)'{/tex}
{tex}\frac14=1-P\left(A\cap B\right){/tex}
{tex}P\left(A\cap B\right)=1-\frac14=\frac34{/tex}
Also, P(A).P(B) = {tex}\frac12×\frac7{12}=\frac7{24}{/tex}
Clearly ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​, {tex}P\left(A\cap B\right)\neq P\left(A\right).P\left(B\right){/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\because{/tex}​​​​​​​ the Events A and B are independent 

 2

OR
(ii)

A box B1 contains 1 white ball and 3 red balls. Another box B2 contains 2 white balls and 3 red balls. If one ball is drawn at random from each of the boxes B1 and B2, then find the probability that the two balls drawn are of the same colour.

Solution:

{tex}P\left(\frac W{B_1}\right)=\frac14,\;P\left(\frac R{B_1}\right)=\frac34{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac W{B_2}\right)=\frac25,\;P\left(\frac R{B_2}\right)=\frac35{/tex}
Probability that both the balls drawn from box B1 and B2 each of same colour
= {tex}P\left(\frac W{B_1}\right)\times P\left(\frac W{B_2}\right)+P\left(\frac R{B_1}\right)\times P\left(\frac R{B_2}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac14\times\frac25+\frac34\times\frac35=\frac2{20}+\frac9{20}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{11}{20}{/tex}

 2
4.

Find the general solution for differential equation:  {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex}

Solution:

We have, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x} e^{-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{-y}\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x{/tex}
Integrating both the sides we get:
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\int\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x+c{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{3}}{3}+c{/tex}
{tex}e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{2}}{3}+c{/tex}

 2
5.

If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 1 and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}, then find {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k,\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex}
{tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
Let {tex}\vec b=x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k{/tex}
As, {tex}\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex}
{tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=1{/tex}
x + y + z = 1 ...(i)
Also, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right)\times\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\left(z-y\right)\hat i-\left(z-x\right)\hat j+\left(y-x\right)\hat k=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
On compairing
z - y = 0, x - z = 1, y - x = -1 ...(ii)
z = y
Putting in eqn (i)
x + 2y = 1
-x + y = -1 (from eqn (ii))
y = 0, z = 0, x = 1
So, {tex}\vec b=\hat i{/tex}
{tex}\vec{\left|b\right|}=\sqrt1=\;1{/tex}

 2
6.

Let X be a random variable which assumes values x1, x2, x3, x4 such that 2P (X = x1) = 3P (X = x2) = P (X = x3) = 5P (X = x4). Find the probability distribution of X.

Solution:

Let P(X = x3) = k Then,
P(x = x1) = {tex}\frac k2{/tex}
P(x = x2) = {tex}\frac k3{/tex}
P(x = x4) = {tex}\frac k5{/tex}
We know that the sum of probabilities in a probability distribution is always 1.
P(X = x​​​​​​1) + P(X = x2) + P(X = x3)+ P(X = x4) = 1
{tex}\frac K2+\frac K3+K+\frac K5=1{/tex}
{tex}\frac{15K+10K+30K+6K}{30}=1{/tex}
61K = 30
K = {tex}\frac{30}{61}{/tex}​​​​​​​

X 1 2 3 4
f(X) {tex}\frac{K}2=\frac{15}{61}{/tex}​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{K}3=\frac{10}{61}{/tex}​​​​​​​ {tex}K=\frac{30}{61}{/tex}​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{K}{5}=\frac6{61}{/tex}​​​​​​​
2

SECTION B

Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.(i)

If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are two vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=|\vec{b}|{/tex}, then prove that {tex}(\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec {a}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|{/tex}
We need to prove {tex}\left(\vec a\right).\left(\vec a+2\vec b\right)=0{/tex}
So, {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|{/tex}
Squaring both sides
{tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|^2=\left|\vec b\right|^2{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec a\right|^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|^2{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec a\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|=0{/tex}
{tex}\vec a(\vec a+2\vec b)=0{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} are unit vectors and {tex}\theta{/tex} is the angle between them, then prove that {tex}\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex}{tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{\left|\vec a\right|^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2-2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|\cos\theta}{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{1^2+1^2-2\times1\times1\times\cos\theta}{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{2-2\cos\theta}=\sqrt{2\left(1-\cos\theta\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} 1 - cos{tex}\theta{/tex} = 2 sin2 {tex}\frac{\theta}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{2\left(2\sin^2\frac\theta2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=2\sin\frac\theta2{/tex}
{tex}\frac12\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sin\frac\theta2{/tex}

 3
8.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex}
Using integration by parts
Taking sin2x as first function
{tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2\int\cos2x.e^xdx{/tex}
{tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2(\cos2x.e^x+\int2\sin2x.e^x)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=e^x.\sin2xdx-2\cos2x.e^x-\int4\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex}
{tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4\int\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex}
Now, from eqn (i)
{tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4I\space+\ C{/tex}
{tex}5I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+\ C{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac15e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+C{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex} 

Solution:

Let I = {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex}.
Put, x2 = t  {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt, we obtain
I = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} 
Using partial fractions,
 {tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{A}{t+1}+\frac{B}{t+2}{/tex} ........(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = A(t + 2) + B(t + 1) ........(ii)
Putting t = - 2 in (ii), we obtain: B = - 1
Putting t = - 1 in (ii), we obtain: A = 1.
Substituting the values of A and B in (i), we obtain
{tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex}   I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)} d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  I = {tex}\int\left(\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}\right) d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  I = log |t + 1| - log |t + 2| + C = log |x2 + 1| - log |x2 + 2| + C

 3
9.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_1^3 \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt{4-x}} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \limits_1^3 \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt{4-x}} d x{/tex} ...(i)
Using property,
{tex}\int_\limits a^bf\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits a^bf\left(a+b-x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits1^3\frac{\sqrt{4-x}}{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}dx{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding eqn (i) and (ii)
{tex}2I=\int_\limits1^3\frac{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}dx{/tex}
{tex}2I=\int_\limits1^3dx=\left[x\right]_1^3{/tex}
{tex}2I=\left[3-1\right]=2{/tex}
I = 1

 3
10.

Find the distance of the point (2, 3, 4) measured along the line {tex}\frac{x-4}{3}=\frac{y+5}{6}=\frac{z+1}{2}{/tex} from the plane 3x + 2y + 2z + 5 = 0.

Solution:

{tex}let\;\frac{x-4}3=\frac{y+5}6=\frac{z+1}2=k{/tex}
So, x = 3k + 4, y = 6k - 5, z = 2k - 1 ...(i)
Putting them in equation of plane
3x + 2y + 2z + 5 = 0
9k + 12 + 12k - 10 + 4k - 2 + 5 = 0
{tex}K=\frac{-1}5{/tex}
Putting in eq. (1)
x = {tex}\frac{17}5{/tex} , y = {tex}\frac{-31}5{/tex}, z = {tex}\frac{-7}5{/tex}
Now, the distance between the points (2, 3, 4) & {tex}\left({\textstyle\frac{17}5},{\textstyle\frac{-31}5},{\textstyle\frac{-7}5}\right){/tex}
{tex}d=\sqrt{\left(x_2-x_1\right)+\left(y_2-y_1\right)+\left(z_2-z_1\right)}{/tex}
{tex}d=\sqrt{\left({\textstyle\frac75}\right)^2+\left({\textstyle\frac{-46}5}\right)^2+\left({\textstyle\frac{-27}5}\right)^2}{/tex}
{tex}d=\sqrt{\left(\frac{49+2116+729}{25}\right)}=\sqrt{\frac{2894}{25}}{/tex}
{tex}d=\frac{53.8}5{/tex} = 10.76 (approx)

 3

SECTION C

Question numbers 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.

Find the area bounded by the curves y = |x - 1| and y = 1, using integration.

Solution:


Required area = {tex}\int_\limits0^1y_1dx+\int_\limits1^2y_2dx{/tex}
= {tex}\int_\limits0^1-\left(x-1\right)dx+\int_\limits1^2\left(x-1\right)dx{/tex}
= {tex}-\left[\frac{x^2}2-x\right]_0^1+\left[\frac{x^2}2-x\right]_1^2{/tex}
= {tex}-\left[\frac12-1\right]+\left[\frac42-2-\frac12+1\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac12+\frac12=1{/tex} sq. unit

 4
12.(i)

Solve the following differential equation: (y-sin2x)dx + tan x dy = 0.

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} (y - sin2x) dx + tan x dy = 0
tan x dy = -(y - sin2x)dx
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(sin^2x-y)}{tanx}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{sin^2x}{tanx}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{y}{tanx}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx} + ycotx = sinx \space cosx{/tex}
It is a differential equation in linear form
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P = cotx, Q = sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I.F = {tex}e^{\int P d x}=e^{\int \cot x d x}{/tex} = {tex}e^{\log |\sin x|}=\sin x{/tex}
So, solution is
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} IF = {tex}\int Q \cdot I F d x+C{/tex},
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sin x = sinx {tex}\cdot{/tex} cosx {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx + c,
y . sinx = {tex}\int \underbrace{\sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x}_I+c{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x d x{/tex}
Let sinx = t, cos x dx = dt
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int t^2 d t=\frac{t^3}{3}+c_2{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2{/tex}
Put in eqn (i)
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2+c_1{/tex}
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c{/tex}
Where (c = c1 + c2)

 4

OR
(ii)

Find the general solution of the differential equation: (x3 + y3)dy = x2ydx

Solution:

(x3 + y3)dy = x2y dx
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{x^2y}{x^3+y^3}{/tex}
It is homogeneous differential equation
Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{vx^3}{x^3+v^3x^3}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac v{1+v^3}{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v-v-v^4}{1+v^3}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1+v^3}{v^4}dv=-\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac1{v^4}dv+\int\frac1vdv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}-\frac1{3v^3}+\log\left|v\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex}
Put v = y/x
{tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|\frac yx\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex}
{tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|y\right|=C{/tex}
This is the required answer.

 4
13.

There are two boxes, namely box-I and box-II. Box-I contains 3 red and 6 black balls. Box-II contains 5 red and 5 black balls. One of the two boxes, is selected at random and a ball is drawn at random. The ball drawn is found to be red. Find the probability that this red ball comes out from box-II.

Solution:

Let E1 be the event when box I is selected
E2 be the event when box II is selected
A be the event of getting red ball in any box
{tex}P\left(E_1\right)=\frac12,\;P\left(E_2\right)=\frac12{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac A{E_1}\right)=\frac39=\frac12{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac A{E_2}\right)=\frac5{10}=\frac12{/tex}
Now, required probability =
{tex}P\left(\frac{E_2}A\right)=\frac{P\left({\frac A{E_2}}\right)P\left(E_2\right)}{P\left({\frac A{E_1}}\right)P\left(E_1\right)+P\left({\frac A{E_2}}\right)P\left(E_2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac{E_2}A\right)=\frac{{\frac12}\times{\frac12}}{{\frac12}\times{\frac13}+{\frac12}\times{\frac12}}=\frac{\frac14}{\frac5{12}}{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac{E_2}A\right)=\frac35{/tex}

 4

Case Study Based Question
14.

Two motorcycles A and B are running at the speed more than the allowed speed on the roads represented by the lines {tex}\vec{r}=\lambda(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=(3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}){/tex} {tex}+\mu(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} respectively.

  1. Find the shortest distance between the given lines. (2)
  2. Find the point at which the motorcycles may collide. (2)
Solution:

  1. Let {tex}\overrightarrow{a_1}=0,\overrightarrow{a_2}=3\widehat i+3\widehat j{/tex}
    {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}=\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k,\overrightarrow{b_2}=2\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex}
    {tex}\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}=3\widehat i+3\widehat j{/tex}
    {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}=\begin{vmatrix}\widehat i&\widehat j&\widehat k\\1&2&-1\\2&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex}
    {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}=3\widehat i-3\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex}
    {tex}\left(a_2-a_1\right)\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)=\left(3\widehat i+3\widehat j\right)\left(3\widehat i-3\widehat j-3\widehat k\right){/tex}
    = 9 - 9 = 0
    Shortest distance between line is
    {tex}d=\left|\frac{\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right).\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right|}\right|{/tex}
    {tex}d=\left|\frac0{3\sqrt3}\right|{/tex} = 0 units.

  2. From equation of both lines, we get
    {tex}\lambda\left(\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k\right)=\left(3\widehat i+3\widehat j\right)+\mu\left(2\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k\right){/tex}
    {tex}\lambda\widehat i+2\lambda\widehat j-\lambda\widehat k=\left(3+2\mu\right)\widehat i+\left(3+\mu\right)\widehat j+\mu\widehat k{/tex}
    On compairing
    {tex}\lambda=3+2\mu{/tex} ...(i)
    {tex}2\lambda=3+\mu{/tex} ...(ii)
    {tex}-\lambda=\mu{/tex} ...(iii)
    From eqn (iii) and (i)
    {tex}\mu=-1{/tex}
    From eqn (ii) and (iii)
    {tex}\lambda=1{/tex}
    {tex}\lambda\left(\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k\right)=\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k{/tex}
    Hence, at point x = 1, y = 2 & z = -1 both motorcycle collides.

 4
answer_key_text: Annual Exam - 2022 Code: 65/5/3 (set-3) Please check that this question paper contains 7 printed pages. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate. Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions. Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period. MATHEMATICS Time allowed:2 hours Maximum Marks:40 General Instructions : Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them : This question paper contains three Sections - A , B and C . Each section is compulsory. Section-A has 6 short-answer type-I questions of 2 marks each. Section-B has 4 short-answer type-II questions of 3 marks each. Section-C has 4 long-answer type questions of 4 marks each. There is an internal choice in some questions. Question 14 is a case study based question with two subparts of 2 marks each. SECTION A Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each. 1. The Cartesian equation of a line AB is: {tex}\frac{2 x-1}{12}=\frac{y+2}{2}=\frac{z-3}{3}{/tex} Find the direction cosines of a line parallel to line AB. Solution: We have, {tex}\frac{2 x-1}{12}=\frac{y+2}{2}=\frac{z-3}{3}{/tex} {tex}\frac{x-{\frac12}}{\frac{12}2}=\frac{y+2}2=\frac{z-3}3{/tex} {tex}\frac{x-{\frac12}}6=\frac{y+2}2=\frac{z-3}3{/tex} The direction ratios of the line parallel to AB are proportional to {tex}\to{/tex} 6, 2, 3 The direction cosines of the line parallel to AB are proportional to {tex}\to{/tex} {tex}\frac6{\sqrt{36+4+9}}=\frac2{\sqrt{36+4+9}}=\frac3{\sqrt{36+4+9}}{/tex} {tex}\frac67=\frac27=\frac37{/tex} 2 2. Find: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{4 x-x^2}}{/tex} Solution: {tex}\int\frac1{\sqrt{4x-x^2}}dx=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x\right)}}dx{/tex} {tex}=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x+4-4\right)}}dx=\int\frac1{-\left(x-2\right)^2+4}dx{/tex} {tex}=\int\frac1{\left(2\right)^2-\left(x-2\right)^2}dx{/tex} Put x - 2 = t, dx = dt {tex}=\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{dx}{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac xa\right)+C{/tex} So, {tex}\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac t2\right)+C{/tex} Putting value of t {tex}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{x-2}2\right)+C{/tex} 2 3. (i) Events A and B are such that P(A) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} , P(B) = {tex}\frac{7}{12}{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{P}(\bar{\mathrm{A}} \cup \bar{\mathrm{B}})=\frac{1}{4}{/tex} Find whether the events A and B are independent or not. Solution: P(A) = {tex}\frac12{/tex} , P(B) = {tex}\frac7{12}{/tex} , {tex}P\left(A'\cup B'\right)=\frac14{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}P\left(A'\cup B'\right)=P\left(A\cap B\right)'{/tex} {tex}\frac14=1-P\left(A\cap B\right){/tex} {tex}P\left(A\cap B\right)=1-\frac14=\frac34{/tex} Also, P(A).P(B) = {tex}\frac12×\frac7{12}=\frac7{24}{/tex} Clearly ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​, {tex}P\left(A\cap B\right)\neq P\left(A\right).P\left(B\right){/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\because{/tex} ​​​​​​​ the Events A and B are independent 2 OR (ii) A box B 1 contains 1 white ball and 3 red balls. Another box B 2 contains 2 white balls and 3 red balls. If one ball is drawn at random from each of the boxes B 1 and B 2 , then find the probability that the two balls drawn are of the same colour. Solution: {tex}P\left(\frac W{B_1}\right)=\frac14,\;P\left(\frac R{B_1}\right)=\frac34{/tex} {tex}P\left(\frac W{B_2}\right)=\frac25,\;P\left(\frac R{B_2}\right)=\frac35{/tex} Probability that both the balls drawn from box B 1 and B 2 each of same colour = {tex}P\left(\frac W{B_1}\right)\times P\left(\frac W{B_2}\right)+P\left(\frac R{B_1}\right)\times P\left(\frac R{B_2}\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac14\times\frac25+\frac34\times\frac35=\frac2{20}+\frac9{20}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{11}{20}{/tex} 2 4. Find the general solution for differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex} Solution: We have, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x} e^{-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{-y}\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x{/tex} Integrating both the sides we get: {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\int\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x+c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{3}}{3}+c{/tex} {tex}e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{2}}{3}+c{/tex} 2 5. If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 1 and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} , then find {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex} Solution: {tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k,\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex} {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} Let {tex}\vec b=x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k{/tex} As, {tex}\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex} {tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=1{/tex} x + y + z = 1 ...(i) Also, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} {tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right)\times\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} {tex}\left(z-y\right)\hat i-\left(z-x\right)\hat j+\left(y-x\right)\hat k=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} On compairing z - y = 0, x - z = 1, y - x = -1 ...(ii) z = y Putting in eqn (i) x + 2y = 1 -x + y = -1 (from eqn (ii)) y = 0, z = 0, x = 1 So, {tex}\vec b=\hat i{/tex} {tex}\vec{\left|b\right|}=\sqrt1=\;1{/tex} 2 6. Let X be a random variable which assumes values x 1 , x 2 , x 3 , x 4 such that 2P (X = x 1 ) = 3P (X = x 2 ) = P (X = x 3 ) = 5P (X = x 4 ). Find the probability distribution of X. Solution: Let P(X = x 3 ) = k Then, P(x = x 1 ) = {tex}\frac k2{/tex} P(x = x 2 ) = {tex}\frac k3{/tex} P(x = x 4 ) = {tex}\frac k5{/tex} We know that the sum of probabilities in a probability distribution is always 1. P(X = x ​​​​​​1 ) + P(X = x 2 ) + P(X = x 3 )+ P(X = x 4 ) = 1 {tex}\frac K2+\frac K3+K+\frac K5=1{/tex} {tex}\frac{15K+10K+30K+6K}{30}=1{/tex} 61K = 30 K = {tex}\frac{30}{61}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ X 1 2 3 4 f(X) {tex}\frac{K}2=\frac{15}{61}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{K}3=\frac{10}{61}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}K=\frac{30}{61}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{K}{5}=\frac6{61}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ 2 SECTION B Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each. 7. (i) If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are two vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=|\vec{b}|{/tex} , then prove that {tex}(\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} . Solution: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|{/tex} We need to prove {tex}\left(\vec a\right).\left(\vec a+2\vec b\right)=0{/tex} So, {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|{/tex} Squaring both sides {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|^2=\left|\vec b\right|^2{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a\right|^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|^2{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\vec a(\vec a+2\vec b)=0{/tex} 3 OR (ii) If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} are unit vectors and {tex}\theta{/tex} is the angle between them, then prove that {tex}\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|{/tex} . Solution: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{\left|\vec a\right|^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2-2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|\cos\theta}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{1^2+1^2-2\times1\times1\times\cos\theta}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{2-2\cos\theta}=\sqrt{2\left(1-\cos\theta\right)}{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} 1 - cos {tex}\theta{/tex} = 2 sin 2 {tex}\frac{\theta}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{2\left(2\sin^2\frac\theta2\right)}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=2\sin\frac\theta2{/tex} {tex}\frac12\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sin\frac\theta2{/tex} 3 8. (i) Find: {tex}\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex} Solution: {tex}I=\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex} Using integration by parts Taking sin2x as first function {tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2\int\cos2x.e^xdx{/tex} {tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2(\cos2x.e^x+\int2\sin2x.e^x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=e^x.\sin2xdx-2\cos2x.e^x-\int4\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex} {tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4\int\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex} Now, from eqn (i) {tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4I\space+\ C{/tex} {tex}5I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+\ C{/tex} {tex}I=\frac15e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+C{/tex} 3 OR (ii) Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex} Solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex} . Put, x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt, we obtain I = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} Using partial fractions, {tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{A}{t+1}+\frac{B}{t+2}{/tex} ........(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = A(t + 2) + B(t + 1) ........(ii) Putting t = - 2 in (ii), we obtain: B = - 1 Putting t = - 1 in (ii), we obtain: A = 1. Substituting the values of A and B in (i), we obtain {tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\left(\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}\right) d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = log |t + 1| - log |t + 2| + C = log |x 2 + 1| - log |x 2 + 2| + C 3 9. Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_1^3 \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt{4-x}} d x{/tex} Solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_1^3 \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt{4-x}} d x{/tex} ...(i) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits a^bf\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits a^bf\left(a+b-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits1^3\frac{\sqrt{4-x}}{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}dx{/tex} ...(ii) Adding eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_\limits1^3\frac{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}dx{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_\limits1^3dx=\left[x\right]_1^3{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[3-1\right]=2{/tex} I = 1 3 10. Find the distance of the point (2, 3, 4) measured along the line {tex}\frac{x-4}{3}=\frac{y+5}{6}=\frac{z+1}{2}{/tex} from the plane 3x + 2y + 2z + 5 = 0. Solution: {tex}let\;\frac{x-4}3=\frac{y+5}6=\frac{z+1}2=k{/tex} So, x = 3k + 4, y = 6k - 5, z = 2k - 1 ...(i) Putting them in equation of plane 3x + 2y + 2z + 5 = 0 9k + 12 + 12k - 10 + 4k - 2 + 5 = 0 {tex}K=\frac{-1}5{/tex} Putting in eq. (1) x = {tex}\frac{17}5{/tex} , y = {tex}\frac{-31}5{/tex} , z = {tex}\frac{-7}5{/tex} Now, the distance between the points (2, 3, 4) & {tex}\left({\textstyle\frac{17}5},{\textstyle\frac{-31}5},{\textstyle\frac{-7}5}\right){/tex} {tex}d=\sqrt{\left(x_2-x_1\right)+\left(y_2-y_1\right)+\left(z_2-z_1\right)}{/tex} {tex}d=\sqrt{\left({\textstyle\frac75}\right)^2+\left({\textstyle\frac{-46}5}\right)^2+\left({\textstyle\frac{-27}5}\right)^2}{/tex} {tex}d=\sqrt{\left(\frac{49+2116+729}{25}\right)}=\sqrt{\frac{2894}{25}}{/tex} {tex}d=\frac{53.8}5{/tex} = 10.76 (approx) 3 SECTION C Question numbers 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each. 11. Find the area bounded by the curves y = |x - 1| and y = 1, using integration. Solution: Required area = {tex}\int_\limits0^1y_1dx+\int_\limits1^2y_2dx{/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^1-\left(x-1\right)dx+\int_\limits1^2\left(x-1\right)dx{/tex} = {tex}-\left[\frac{x^2}2-x\right]_0^1+\left[\frac{x^2}2-x\right]_1^2{/tex} = {tex}-\left[\frac12-1\right]+\left[\frac42-2-\frac12+1\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac12+\frac12=1{/tex} sq. unit 4 12. (i) Solve the following differential equation: (y-sin 2 x)dx + tan x dy = 0. Solution: {tex}\because{/tex} (y - sin 2 x) dx + tan x dy = 0 tan x dy = -(y - sin 2 x)dx {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(sin^2x-y)}{tanx}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{sin^2x}{tanx}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{y}{tanx}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx} + ycotx = sinx \space cosx{/tex} It is a differential equation in linear form {tex}\therefore{/tex} P = cotx, Q = sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I.F = {tex}e^{\int P d x}=e^{\int \cot x d x}{/tex} = {tex}e^{\log |\sin x|}=\sin x{/tex} So, solution is y {tex}\cdot{/tex} IF = {tex}\int Q \cdot I F d x+C{/tex} , y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sin x = sinx {tex}\cdot{/tex} cosx {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx + c, y . sinx = {tex}\int \underbrace{\sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x}_I+c{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x d x{/tex} Let sinx = t, cos x dx = dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int t^2 d t=\frac{t^3}{3}+c_2{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2{/tex} Put in eq n (i) y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2+c_1{/tex} y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c{/tex} Where (c = c 1 + c 2 ) 4 OR (ii) Find the general solution of the differential equation: (x 3 + y 3 )dy = x 2 ydx Solution: (x 3 + y 3 )dy = x 2 y dx {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{x^2y}{x^3+y^3}{/tex} It is homogeneous differential equation Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{vx^3}{x^3+v^3x^3}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac v{1+v^3}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v-v-v^4}{1+v^3}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1+v^3}{v^4}dv=-\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\int\frac1{v^4}dv+\int\frac1vdv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}-\frac1{3v^3}+\log\left|v\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex} Put v = y/x {tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|\frac yx\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex} {tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|y\right|=C{/tex} This is the required answer. 4 13. There are two boxes, namely box-I and box-II. Box-I contains 3 red and 6 black balls. Box-II contains 5 red and 5 black balls. One of the two boxes, is selected at random and a ball is drawn at random. The ball drawn is found to be red. Find the probability that this red ball comes out from box-II. Solution: Let E 1 be the event when box I is selected E 2 be the event when box II is selected A be the event of getting red ball in any box {tex}P\left(E_1\right)=\frac12,\;P\left(E_2\right)=\frac12{/tex} {tex}P\left(\frac A{E_1}\right)=\frac39=\frac12{/tex} {tex}P\left(\frac A{E_2}\right)=\frac5{10}=\frac12{/tex} Now, required probability = {tex}P\left(\frac{E_2}A\right)=\frac{P\left({\frac A{E_2}}\right)P\left(E_2\right)}{P\left({\frac A{E_1}}\right)P\left(E_1\right)+P\left({\frac A{E_2}}\right)P\left(E_2\right)}{/tex} {tex}P\left(\frac{E_2}A\right)=\frac{{\frac12}\times{\frac12}}{{\frac12}\times{\frac13}+{\frac12}\times{\frac12}}=\frac{\frac14}{\frac5{12}}{/tex} {tex}P\left(\frac{E_2}A\right)=\frac35{/tex} 4 Case Study Based Question 14. Two motorcycles A and B are running at the speed more than the allowed speed on the roads represented by the lines {tex}\vec{r}=\lambda(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=(3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}){/tex} {tex}+\mu(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} respectively. Find the shortest distance between the given lines. (2) Find the point at which the motorcycles may collide. (2) Solution: Let {tex}\overrightarrow{a_1}=0,\overrightarrow{a_2}=3\widehat i+3\widehat j{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}=\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k,\overrightarrow{b_2}=2\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}=3\widehat i+3\widehat j{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}=\begin{vmatrix}\widehat i&\widehat j&\widehat k\\1&2&-1\\2&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}=3\widehat i-3\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\left(a_2-a_1\right)\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)=\left(3\widehat i+3\widehat j\right)\left(3\widehat i-3\widehat j-3\widehat k\right){/tex} = 9 - 9 = 0 Shortest distance between line is {tex}d=\left|\frac{\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right).\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right|}\right|{/tex} {tex}d=\left|\frac0{3\sqrt3}\right|{/tex} = 0 units. From equation of both lines, we get {tex}\lambda\left(\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k\right)=\left(3\widehat i+3\widehat j\right)+\mu\left(2\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k\right){/tex} {tex}\lambda\widehat i+2\lambda\widehat j-\lambda\widehat k=\left(3+2\mu\right)\widehat i+\left(3+\mu\right)\widehat j+\mu\widehat k{/tex} On compairing {tex}\lambda=3+2\mu{/tex} ...(i) {tex}2\lambda=3+\mu{/tex} ...(ii) {tex}-\lambda=\mu{/tex} ...(iii) From eqn (iii) and (i) {tex}\mu=-1{/tex} From eqn (ii) and (iii) {tex}\lambda=1{/tex} {tex}\lambda\left(\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k\right)=\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k{/tex} Hence, at point x = 1, y = 2 & z = -1 both motorcycle collides. 4
